name: auth-arch-refactor
description: Detects published packages that consume the reactor-api surfaces changed by the AUTH_REVIEW A1-A8 architecture cleanups: DocumentPermissionService decision methods moved into the authorization strategy (A1), the getParentIds per-call parameter and its duplicate factories removed (A2), the GraphQLManager authConfig constructor parameter replaced by a shared AuthService instance (A6), and the isSupremeAdmin/canCreate semantics split (A8). Findings inside @powerhousedao/reactor-api itself are the source of these symbols, not consumers.

rules.description: Call/reference to a DocumentPermissionService decision method removed by A1 (canWriteDocument, canManageDocument, filterReadableDocuments). Decisions now live on IAuthorizationService.

Call to .canReadDocument — removed from DocumentPermissionService by A1, but BaseSubgraph keeps a protected helper with the same name that delegates to the authorization service. Triage the receiver: this.canReadDocument(...) on a subgraph is fine; documentPermissionService.canReadDocument(...) is broken.
Call to .canExecuteOperation — removed from DocumentPermissionService by A1 (decision moved to IAuthorizationService.canMutate; raw ACL existence check renamed hasOperationGrant).
Reference to createGetParentIdsFn (duplicate exports deleted by A2) or BaseSubgraph.getParentIdsFn (removed; the parent resolver is injected into the authorization service at construction).

Call to .canRead/.canWrite/.canManage/.canMutate. Two changes share these names: IAuthorizationService signatures lose the optional trailing getParentIds parameter (A2; extra args are ignored at runtime, two-arg calls unaffected), and DocumentPermissionService.canRead/canWrite(documentId, user, getParentIds) are REMOVED entirely (A1; calls on a DocumentPermissionService receiver throw at runtime). Triage by receiver.
Direct construction of GraphQLManager — the 11th constructor parameter changes from AuthConfig to a shared AuthService instance (A6).
Call to isSupremeAdmin — semantics documented/refined by A8 (returns true for everyone under the OPEN policy). Behavior unchanged; flagged for awareness.
